sous_titres.text: That's a week from tomorrow. Put it on your calendar.
Well, I don't think I got an invitation.
Unless it got lost in the junk mail.
So much junk mail coming in all the time.
Have you seen the fake handwriting they have now?
Looks like someone really wrote it by hand.
I haven't had time to send anything out.
My guests have come wandering through again.
They'd love to have an outing, I'm sure.
I don't like that they're staying with you again.
They like it here. It's a good spot for them to stop.
Don't they have anywhere else they can stay?
Feels like they're taking advantage of you.
I'm gonna stop by this week and make sure
they don't have you tied up in the basement.
Nothing as exciting as that, I assure you.
Though that would possibly be quite enjoyable.
I could do without their dog though, not a well-trained creature.
Keeps mistaking the corner of my couch for a tree.
Been hoping to find a good tire for this tree for ages.
Jo, the water situation is getting worse.
Just a few minutes of lukewarm and then cold.
Just got to get through this week first.
Shouldn't even be here right now. I've got so much to do.
I do, too. And I don't know what I'm supposed to do without hot water.
Lizzy, I told you, you can use my shower.
I want my own water working.
I'll be free to deal with it after that.
I have a show too, you know. I'm just...
You're not the only one with a deadline.
I know, but I have two shows which is insane.
Well, you have to wait. You're out of food.
I'll go to the store later.
Shut up! God, Ricky, you're ruining my workday.
This is Todd-a-phonic time. Happy Monday to you all.
Counting the days until the Quazi show on the roof of the...
A whole year of my life.
What did you end up doing with the zipper?
Well, I abandoned the zipper idea and went with Velcro.
Which, like you said, is not great
- Come here, let me help. - because it sticks to the yarn.
Yeah, it gets a little messy, but...
Hey, Lizzy, can I get a little bit of coffee, please?
Could you at least tell me who's on my board?
Look, Randal, he shouldn't be on my board.
We got, like, different theories of cultural production.
Or he just doesn't like me. I don't know.
I'm just putting that out there.
"She sees glass as a religious vessel for personal mastic meditation."
"The dream like glass would seem like..."
Let's get the latest end-of-year schedule out today.
Oh, these are travel receipts for our guests.
And I gave Tim Hunter cash, so the invoice is in there.
Lizzy, you ready to fire this week?
There'll be a few stragglers though, is that okay?
We'll make room. Just bring by what you have.
It's all right. We'll make it work.
Oh, I saw some of Jo's work at her studio yesterday.
Wow, it just gives me such a lift.
She'd be more amazing if she got my hot water working.
Hey, Lizzy, I've gotta get to class.
Bring your stuff by. We'll make it work.
Media heat. The heat of the technology.
So, what does it all do?
Does somebody wanna go on the inside?
I'm not gonna lock you in.
This one, doesn't make me cringe.
I had a special feeling about this one. You know, it's...
it's right where I wanted to be.
Good form, but not too slick.
You know you're meeting with the dean at noon?
She knows. And I was wondering
if it's okay if I take tomorrow off work?
I was thinking I might do that.
I'd have a lot of work.
Lizzy, if you're taking a personal day
- Okay, so I won't be in tomorrow. - I got it.
And have you talked to Dad?
Because those people are back at his house again.
The people who stay with him.
Well, your father is living his own life.
And we all wish him the very best.
I can't figure out what class this is.
But I really want to join it.
I haven't gotten to read the article yet
I want to impress those students.
And the flyer was very nice.
...in store for us and I can't wait to listen.
But while I wait to listen, let's listen to...
This is, like, the Mike Bloomfield, Al Kooper super session...
My show opens on Monday. It's at Lauren's again.
If you're not busy, maybe you'll come.
They said they were doing a catalogue and now they're like
"Oh, it won't be ready before the opening."
Only our school would do that. God.
If there's no catalogue, it's just my thesis show all over again.
Things usually get done, just not on time.
Go somewhere else. Go die somewhere else.
I found a bird. Check it out.
I think it was mauled by a cat.
It was flapping around out back in the flowerbed.
His wing is messed up. Broken, I think.
I think it was Oscar who did it.
That cat is a menace. They really need to keep him inside.
Come on, let's fix this guy up.
I think this should be good.
I probably made this Freshman year.
What are we gonna do with you, huh?
Hey, Lizzy, what are you doing today?
Working in the studio from home. I got a lot to do.
I have to install at Nationale.
Ricky is upstairs, the bird can hang with you in the studio.
I took today off to work.
Aw, this guy is no trouble.
I'll get him back from you as soon as I get home.
Oh, yeah, you've done enough already.
Hi, Jo, your bird is acting weird.
I don't think it's breathing right.
What, are you having a heart attack or something?
So much to do today, Mr. Bird, you're not helping.
Looks like somebody did a good job with the bandaging.
I'm not going to bother him.
Have you been keeping him warm?
Birds like to be kept warm.
I work in a garage studio. It's not cold.
I've been glazing down there, but that's not too stinky.
Well, maybe the studio is not the best place.
You could put a hot water bottle in the box.
Get one at the drug store. Fill it with hot water.
No hot water at my place. Going on two weeks now.
Heat some water in a kettle or a pot, just wrap it in a towel.
You don't want it too hot, but comfortable.
I put it on my credit card. I need to be paid back.
$150? Wow, what'd they do to him?
It's my one big day for working
and it got totally screwed up taking care of your bird.
You can take the money off the rent.
Doesn't get me back the time.
Anything else I should know? About the bird?
Okay. Thanks for taking care of him.
You're coming over to my place. We'll have a nice time.
I had to pull an all-nighter.
You can take them up to cone six.
How many more pieces we got coming?
Got him over by the window.
I'm gonna take him to the office. It's loud in here.
The vet thinks it has a broken wing and is stressed out.
Tell Ned he can pick it up in my office.
You took a pigeon to a vet?
Okay, go, thank you. You're off duty.
We shoot pigeons with BB guns. We don't take them to the vet.
Yo, Lizzy took a pigeon to the vet.
She thinks the pigeon is stressed out.
Before he journeyed in his spaceship to the Moon in 1971
Starfield astronaut Allan Shepard didn't think he'd be...
- For you. - Thank you, come in.
My captors are dying to meet you.
So, this is Lizzy. Wonderful to meet you.
Dorothy and Lee have just come down from Canada.
Yeah, we were standing in the gardens of Victoria.
You were there when you were little.
I must go to the toilette.
Oh, Bill, shall Lee make some coffee?
You're about out of coffee by the way.
And we're running low on 1% milk, if we're making a list.
Oh, that's your middle of the night cereal eating.
Dad, how long are they staying?
Well, where do they live anyway?
They winter in Mexico and summer in Canada
If you see anything you like that you want
by all means, just take it.
You should make more like this.
Well, you should do it anyway.
I do a little of this, a little of that
it's time to watch TV again.
must just got up and walked away.
Come on, let's go join our friends again.
- You barely know those people. - Well, look at you.
You've been there all the time.
Here, would you give this to your brother
For five years, he lived in that jacket.
I mean, it was mine, but he took possession.
I talk to him all the time.
- C'mon, let's go... - When's... When's the last time
He brought a delicious stuffing for Thanksgiving.
Dad... Dad, that was six months ago.
Well, he was great six months ago. And his stuffing was superb.
I didn't know you did this.
Hello, Jo. How are you, dear?
Hi, Jean. I'm good. I think so, anyway.
I think I'm done here and there.
I think the shows speak to each other in a really cool way.
The Abigail Firschein Memorial Show, it's really such a big deal and...
The... The first OCAC alum they've ever featured.
I hope the catalogue comes together in time.
Oh, we are thrilled to be honoring you.
Did you know Jo Tran is getting a catalogue?
Hey, I can take the bird.
I'm gonna go get something to eat with Jackie and then go home.
He can hang in the car for a bit.
I'll drop him off when I come home.
Okay, I'll take him later then.
You're coming to my opening tomorrow, right?
Lizzy, do you have any dental floss?
Hey, Jim, can you let me into the guest bathroom?
You're not supposed to be in there.
My hot water is still broken. I really need a shower.
I'll get the key, but you're not supposed to be in there.
I got your door unlocked. I don't want any messes in there.
It's from Dad's. He thought you'd like it.
And here's an invite to my show, too, if you want.
- I left you a message. - Yeah.
You know I'm not a big message guy.
Thought I'd be here earlier, but so much traffic.
People don't know how to drive.
Yeah, I almost got hit the other day.
It's the only channel I like to watch.
Has all the old shows on it.
The good stuff. But now it's just gone.
I don't... I don't know if that's something they can really do.
Those people have their fingers all over this place.
Hey, I saw your car and I thought I'd say "Hi".
Oh, hey. I thought you were the other neighbors.
Sean was just telling me about his TV reception
Thinks the neighbors have something to do with it.
See what you're trying to pass along. Ha-ha.
If you want, I could get you a box set of The Twilight Zone.
That's not the same if I know what's coming on.
Oh, yeah, I remember that jacket.
Is he still doing that landscaping thing?
I thought he had some work.
I don't think so. Not for a while.
Oh, he has his struggles. I am aware.
He's a genius is part of the problem.
Always a few steps ahead of everyone.
Just makes it so hard to know what to expect.
I don't know that he's a genius.
A lot of people say that. They always have.
He was always incredibly creative and...
some of the things he's done, just...
A lot of people are creative.
You should give him a little more credit.
I do give him credit. I just think he has problems.
Go... you're running for your lives.
- Bye, Marlene. - See ya. Thank you.
Come to slum around in studio arts?
Oh, no. No, just hiding, really.
You can hide in here with me.
Jo bought an old apartment building.
Dad and her brother helped her fix it up.
Yeah, she really figured it out.
She can work in the studio all the time now.
I saw you have a show coming up.
I have a friend in town from New York.
Sorry I couldn't be there, but I saw the room. It looked amazing.
I talked to Marlene Heyman for a long time last night.
Went on and on about your girls, the colors.
It is cool. She's having a moment.
Anyway, I can take the bird off your hands.
Hey, Jo, now that your shows are open
you'll get on the water heater, right?
'Cause I'd really like to take a shower.
All right, this one turned out beautiful.
Must've been burning hot on one side.
It's a little funky, but I don't mind imperfections.
In fact, I like them. I prefer it.
I was gonna show you something.
But you look sort of busy.
What does it look like I'm doing?
I didn't know you were into earth work.
Poetry is the voice of the earth.
You have to listen to what isn't being said.
So many people choose not to hear.
Things are not good here. He's not good. He's really off...
Still in the backyard, I think.
- I can... I can go with you. - No, please. Lizzy...
- Best thing I've ever seen. - Yeah.
I mean, it was just a momentary thing.
Oh, really, just, like, the worst.
But I think you should look at her earlier work
because, I think, that's actually what...
This idea of, like, post feminism ideal.
Oh, I would. I think it's actually a pretty good piece, come on.
- I need to see it. - Yeah, yeah.
what's going on over there? Can you fill me in?
Hey, one of your guests took my parking space. That is not cool.
that the bird needs his paper changed every few hours.
Gets pretty gross in there if you don't change the paper.
Mom, I'm really anxious to know what's going on there.
You know, I'm so fucking sick of not having hot water, Jo.
It is such a total drag.
And such a shitty thing to do to a person. I'm sick of it.
I need to use the shower, too.
That shower is a storage space. It's packed.
Well, I don't have any hot water and I need a shower.
What's going on? Why haven't you called me back?
I just left. He was finally sleeping.
Are you sure that he's asleep?
The hot water bottle doesn't even stay hot
When's my hot water coming back on?
Well, you keep saying that, but...
I have to drive out to Kelso to get the new heater
and the guy said he won't be there tomorrow.
But he's supposed to be there the next day.
Is that the only water heater anywhere?
Why does it have to be that one?
It's the best and cheapest, yeah.
I said you don't pay that much rent, that's all.
You have a really good deal. It's no excuse for the water.
And, please, stop leaving me messages like that.
This is a lot of cheese
and I don't think that people are gonna eat this much.
They'll eat a little bit of it
and then the rest of it is gonna go to waste.
Lizzy, your guests are gonna be here in a few minutes.
It's going to be a special night
and no one is going to be looking at the cheese.
Okay, they're coming to celebrate you and all of your beautiful work.
I just wanted to let you know
He said he'd come tonight, and so I'm bringing him along as my date.
Okay, if you think he really wants to.
Of course he does. So, we'll see you soon.
He's not gonna break anything, is he?
Seems like you're not taking away any cheese.
It's close to downtown, that's nice.
I mean, it might not look like much
but they do some really great things here.
Why don't you guys have a look? Or...
Ooh, don't mind if I do.
- Thank you. Thank you, thank you. - Zinfandel...
Well, you are very daring with the colors.
I was hoping to find Sean back here.
Oh. I'm to pick him up and I wonder where he is.
Sometimes he... he walks through the park
over to the corner store on 92nd.
You know, it's just a funny thing. A decade makes all the difference.
Yes, and who's gonna tell... How they're gonna tell their stories?
When I'm in my dream space
that's when it happens. I mean, like...
I just flow, you know? I can do no wrong in that.
At that point, it just happens.
Janet is the gallery director where I show in New York.
Well, my best piece got closer to the fire than I wanted
but I didn't have any control over that.
Well, things look great from here.
Come on, at this stage, you've just got to own it.
How often does something come out of the kiln
- You came. - Of course I did.
I brought this guy. He wanted to say hi.
He's seen you make it all.
Have you swum with the dolphins?
I mean, she was just a snob.
Such a snob. Oh, my God. A first-class snob.
Your dad is talking about Hilda Morris.
I just, kind of, fell into that.
My pal did the cabinets in her living room
and he said she didn't have a good word for anybody.
Lizzy, it looks wonderful, just wonderful.
This is Lizzy's mom, Jean Carr.
The other thing about her was this leather vest.
She would not be seen without her leather vest.
- Yes, I am. - You never knew Hilda Morris.
No, I don't think you did.
- Mom, where's Sean? - I don't know.
You know this other friend of mine has a house...
I thought you were getting him.
And I... I thought he might be here.
He shouldn't just be out by himself. Mom, that's not good.
I'm sure he's fine. He's probably at a friend's.
Mom, he could be anywhere right now.
This is your night. Try to enjoy yourself.
Well, I'm not just gonna stand here while Sean has gone missing.
I... I went to pick you up.
I said I was coming. I took the bus.
I thought we said that I was getting you.
Try picking up your phone once in a while.
It's for everyone. I don't want to run out.
Cheese is out on the table.
You shouldn't put cheese out on the table
if you don't want people to eat it.
Sean, have you spoken to Craig?
Yeah, they came to the Derick Tibbs show.
I think they do it on purpose. Kind of just come.
Hey, Lizzy, I... I just wanted to tell you
I... I really like your friend Janet.
And... and she said she wants to come and see
my pots, if you know what I'm saying.
Oh, my God, did you say that to her?
No, I didn't have to say anything. She offered.
That was to get you to leave.
No, it was not to get me to leave, Jean.
We had a meeting of the minds, all right?
We have a lot in common.
Well, we do... As it turns out, we do.
You're not supposed to have any cheese.
Who the hell are you? I can eat as much as I want.
Oh, Lizzy, the show is awesome.
People sure love to kiss your ass, huh?
- You're embarrassing me. - All right, all right, all right.
You don't have to tell them how to talk to each other.
What I say to them doesn't
- really concern you. - Concerns me in that I'm here
- and I have to listen to it. - No one, but you in there, Bill.
No, you're standing in your own little world, Jean.
Listen, buddy, don't you begin to tell me how to talk to my kids.
I guess he was ready to go.
Gonna go to get some cigarettes.
I heard he's doing deserts again.
Yeah... I liked his trees better.
I always forget Mike's color blind. Isn't that weird?
